title: Severe shortness of breath in a 16-year-old
presentation: A 16-year-old male with known asthma presents with progressive shortness of breath for 3 hours that is not responding to his home albuterol inhaler. He has used it 6 times today. Vitals: BP 128/82, HR 124, RR 32, Temp 37.2C, SpO2 91%. Exam: audible wheeze, accessory muscle use, speaks in 2-3 word sentences, prolonged expiratory phase.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Speaks in short sentences + SpO2 91% + accessory muscle use = severe exacerbation. Back-to-back albuterol + steroids immediately. Silent chest = impending respiratory failure.
